purpose: Public-facing repository README for onboarding users to Synapse, explaining installation, launch, command usage, major features, and best practices. Key content: [![Electron](https://img.shields.io/badge/Electron-desktop%20app-47848F?logo=electron&logoColor=white)]() [![React](https://img.shields.io/badge/React%2019-UI-61DAFB?logo=react&logoColor=black)]() [![Claude Code](https://img.shields.io/badge/Claude%20Code-compatible-blueviolet?logo=anthropic&logoColor=white)]() [![License: Source Available](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-Source%20Available-orange.svg)](LICENSE) The file is 796 lines long and should be treated as a knowledge/control-plane source rather than application code.
gotchas: Command-count drift risk: current filesystem has 35 _commands/Synapse markdown commands and 24 _commands/project markdown commands, plus 15 profiles; hard-coded command counts in docs can become stale quickly.
Guide description may be narrow: `!guide` remains a command-decision aid, but the app also exposes an in-app markdown Guide modal backed by documentation/guide via GuideService and IPC.
README is user-facing marketing/onboarding; keep it accurate but concise, and verify feature claims against package.json and electron/src files rather than copying older documentation counts.
patterns: Control-plane documentation pattern: markdown is treated as operational configuration for agents, not just explanatory prose.
Uses fenced code blocks for command examples, schemas, or prompt templates.
Uses markdown tables for compact command, file, or option matrices.
conventions: Markdown headings use a single H1 followed by H2/H3 sections for navigable documentation.
ASCII double hyphen is used in prose where many docs avoid Unicode punctuation for terminal readability.
Shell examples use fenced `bash` code blocks.
